content.thinking:
Now let me understand the current setup:
- 3 mongo-rs pods (mongo-rs-0, mongo-rs-1, mongo-rs-2) running MongoDB 6.0 without TLS
- An openssl-toolbox pod available to generate certificates
- A headless service "mongo" in the mongodb namespace

I need to:
1. Generate CA key and certificate
2. Generate server certificate (for the MongoDB servers)
3. Generate client certificate
4. Store these in a Kubernetes Secret
5. Update the StatefulSet to mount /etc/tls with the certificates
6. Reconfigure MongoDB to use TLS

Let me check what tools are available in the openssl-toolbox pod and examine the current MongoDB configuration.

input.command: kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- openssl version 2>&1

content.thinking:
Good. Now I'll generate the certificates in the openssl-toolbox and then create a Kubernetes Secret with them, then update the StatefulSet.

The plan:
1. Generate a CA key and self-signed CA certificate
2. Generate a server key and CSR, sign it with the CA - the server cert needs to have SANs for all mongo-rs pod hostnames:
   - mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
   - mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
   - mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
3. Create server.pem by combining the server key and certificate, since MongoDB expects them together
4. Generate a client key and CSR, then sign it with the CA
5. Create client.pem by combining the client key and certificate
6. Store ca.crt, server.pem, and client.pem in a Kubernetes Secret
7. Update the StatefulSet to mount the secret at /etc/tls and add --tlsMode requireTLS (or allowTLS initially), --tlsCertificateKeyFile /etc/tls/server.pem, and --tlsCAFile /etc/tls/ca.crt

Now I'll generate the certificates step by step, keeping in mind that MongoDB 6.0 uses --tlsMode rather than --sslMode for these configuration options.

content.text: Good. Now I'll generate CA, server, and client certificates in the toolbox pod, then wire everything up.

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
mkdir -p /tmp/tls && cd /tmp/tls

# Generate CA key and self-signed cert
openssl genrsa -out ca.key 4096
openssl req -new -x509 -days 3650 -key ca.key -out ca.crt \
  -subj "/CN=MongoDB-CA/O=KARMA"

echo "=== CA generated ==="
' 2>&1
input.description: Generate CA key and certificate in toolbox

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp/tls

# Create server SAN config covering all replica set members + localhost
cat > server-ext.cnf << EOF
[req]
distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name
req_extensions = v3_req
[req_distinguished_name]
[v3_req]
subjectAltName = @alt_names
[alt_names]
DNS.1 = mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.2 = mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.3 = mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.4 = mongo-rs-0.mongo
DNS.5 = mongo-rs-1.mongo
DNS.6 = mongo-rs-2.mongo
DNS.7 = localhost
IP.1 = 127.0.0.1
EOF

# Generate server key and CSR
openssl genrsa -out server.key 4096
openssl req -new -key server.key -out server.csr \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-rs/O=KARMA" \
  -config server-ext.cnf

# Sign server cert with CA, including SANs
openssl x509 -req -in server.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key \
  -CAcreateserial -out server.crt -days 3650 \
  -extfile server-ext.cnf -extensions v3_req

# server.pem = key + cert (MongoDB format)
cat server.key server.crt > server.pem

echo "=== Server cert generated ==="
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt server.crt
' 2>&1
input.description: Generate server key, CSR, and signed certificate with SANs

content.content:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN = mongo-rs, O = KARMA
=== Server cert generated ===
server.crt: OK

tool_use_result.stdout:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN = mongo-rs, O = KARMA
=== Server cert generated ===
server.crt: OK

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp/tls

# Generate client key, CSR, and signed cert
openssl genrsa -out client.key 4096
openssl req -new -key client.key -out client.csr \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-client/O=KARMA"
openssl x509 -req -in client.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key \
  -CAcreateserial -out client.crt -days 3650

# client.pem = key + cert
cat client.key client.crt > client.pem

echo "=== Client cert generated ==="
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt client.crt

echo "=== Files ==="
ls -la /tmp/tls/
' 2>&1

content.thinking: Now I need to extract the certificate files from the toolbox pod and create a Kubernetes Secret. Let me extract the files.

content.text: All three certs verified. Now pull them out and create a Kubernetes Secret.

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/tls/ca.crt > /tmp/ca.crt
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/tls/server.pem > /tmp/server.pem
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/tls/client.pem > /tmp/client.pem
echo "Files pulled. Sizes:"
wc -c /tmp/ca.crt /tmp/server.pem /tmp/client.pem
input.description: Pull certificate files from toolbox pod to local tmp

input.command:
kubectl create secret generic mongo-tls \
  -n mongodb \
  --from-file=ca.crt=/tmp/ca.crt \
  --from-file=server.pem=/tmp/server.pem \
  --from-file=client.pem=/tmp/client.pem 2>&1
input.description: Create mongo-tls Secret in mongodb namespace

content.text: Now patch the StatefulSet to mount the secret at `/etc/tls` and enable `--tlsMode requireTLS`:

input.description: Patch StatefulSet to enable TLS and mount certificates

input.command:
# Test 1: Plain connection MUST fail
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongo-rs-0 -- \
  mongosh --quiet --eval "db.runCommand({ping:1})" \
  --host localhost --port 27017 2>&1 | head -20

content.content:
MongoServerSelectionError: connection <monitor> to 127.0.0.1:27017 closed
command terminated with exit code 1

tool_use_result.stdout:
MongoServerSelectionError: connection <monitor> to 127.0.0.1:27017 closed
command terminated with exit code 1

input.command:
# Test 2: TLS connection MUST succeed
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongo-rs-0 -- \
  mongosh --quiet \
  --tls \
  --tlsCAFile /etc/tls/ca.crt \
  --tlsCertificateKeyFile /etc/tls/client.pem \
  --host localhost --port 27017 \
  --eval "db.runCommand({ping:1})" 2>&1

content.thinking:
Both tests passed:
1. Plain connection failed with `MongoServerSelectionError: connection <monitor> to 127.0.0.1:27017 closed` - expected behavior when `--tlsMode requireTLS` is set
2. TLS connection succeeded with `ok: 1`

Let me also check the replica set status via TLS to make sure it's still healthy.

content.text: Both tests pass. Let me also verify the replica set is healthy over TLS:

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongo-rs-0 -- \
  mongosh --quiet \
  --tls \
  --tlsCAFile /etc/tls/ca.crt \
  --tlsCertificateKeyFile /etc/tls/client.pem \
  --host localhost --port 27017 \
  --eval "JSON.stringify(rs.status().members.map(m => ({name: m.name, state: m.stateStr})))" 2>&1
input.description: Check replica set member health via TLS
